title: clematis acutangula
intro: Clematis acutangula, also known as Chinese clematis or kee, is a perennial flowering plant native to China and India, recognized by its distinctive, fragrant flowers and edible roots. Traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Chinese medicine, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits associated with Clematis acutangula include its potential to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and vitality.
